─[ fastembed-local-files-only ]─ ┐

name: fastembed-local-files-only description: fastembed phones home to HuggingFace by default even with a baked model cache user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Read, Edit

fastembed-local-files-only

When using fastembed’s TextEmbedding class with a pre-downloaded model (e.g. baked into a Docker image), you must pass local_files_only=True to prevent network requests to HuggingFace.

Snags

  • Default behavior phones home: TextEmbedding(model_name="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5") attempts to reach HuggingFace Hub to check for model updates, even when the model is fully cached in FASTEMBED_CACHE_PATH. This causes SSL errors or timeouts in air-gapped, restricted, or corporate network environments.

  • The fix is a kwarg, not an env var: The local_files_only parameter is passed through kwargs to the parent class and stored as self._local_files_only. There is no environment variable equivalent.

# Bad — phones home even with cached model
model = TextEmbedding(model_name="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5")

# Good — uses only the local cache
model = TextEmbedding(model_name="BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5", local_files_only=True)

  • Baking the model at build time is not enough: The Dockerfile RUN python -c "from fastembed import TextEmbedding; TextEmbedding(...)" downloads and caches the model, but runtime code still needs local_files_only=True or it will try to reach HuggingFace on every cold start.

  • The parameter is not in the constructor signature: It’s consumed via **kwargs and popped in a parent class (self._local_files_only = kwargs.pop("local_files_only", False)). IDE autocompletion won’t show it.

PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
